source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Crane Structural Damage

actions_taken: Crane Operator stopped the job upon hearing loud noise
Crane boom returned to boom rest and placed out of service
BSEE Inspection Team assembled onsite on 29-DEC-2015
event.summary: On 16-DEC-2015 at approximately 1845 hours, a crane incident occurred at Fieldwood Energy's Vermillion 261-A-Aux facility during boom positioning activities. The boom hoist limit device failed to function, allowing the boom to exceed its maximum angle height and contact the boom stops, resulting in bent boom chords and bent boom stops. Contributing factors included failure of pre-use inspection to identify a loose and bent extension shaft on the boom hoist limit device, a boom angle indicator obscured by paint overspray and grime, and failure of the job safety analysis to identify hazards associated with close proximity of load to crane's minimum boom radius and low lighting conditions.
key_phrases: boom angle indicator covered with paint overspray and grime
job safety analysis failed to identify deficiencies
pre-use inspection failed to identify deficiencies
Signal Person and Crane Operator did not notice boom nearing stops

hazards.name: Crane boom exceeding maximum angle height
Low lighting conditions during lifting operations
Load positioned in close proximity to crane minimum boom radius

threats.name: Loose and bent extension shaft on boom hoist limit device
Obscured boom angle indicator preventing operator awareness
Failure to detect boom approaching maximum angle during positioning

controls.name: Boom hoist limit device
controls.barrier_role: Automatic mechanical stop to prevent boom from exceeding maximum angle
controls.lod_basis: Primary engineered control to prevent boom overtravel

supporting_text: The boom reached the upper boom hoist limit device but it failed to function; most likely because the extension shaft was loose and bent thus allowing the boom to exceed its maximum angle height and come into contact with the boom stops.
Boom hoist limit device failed to automatically stop the boom hoist winch when the boom reached its maximum predetermined high angle.

controls.name: Pre-use inspection
controls.barrier_role: Identify equipment deficiencies before lifting operations
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to detect equipment defects prior to use

supporting_text: Although a job safety analysis and a pre-use inspection were performed prior to the lifting activities, they failed to identify any deficiencies with the crane
The pre-use inspection failed to identify the extension shaft for the boom hoist limit device was loose and bent.

controls.name: Boom angle indicator
controls.barrier_role: Provide visual feedback to crane operator on boom angle position
controls.lod_basis: Operator awareness control to monitor boom position

supporting_text: The boom angle indicator was covered with paint overspray and grime making it difficult to read from the Crane Operator's station.
Boom angle indicator is not readable from the operator's station. The boom angle indicator is faded and has paint overspray.

controls.name: Job safety analysis
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and implement precautionary measures prior to lifting
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to identify and mitigate lifting hazards

supporting_text: During the job safety analysis, all parties failed to identify the associated hazards of the lift due to the close proximity of the load to the crane's minimum boom radius and the low light conditions

Although a job safety analysis and a pre-use inspection were performed prior to the lifting activities, they failed to identify any deficiencies with the crane nor did they note the potential hazard associated with the close proximity of the load to the crane's minimum boom radius and low lighting conditions.

controls.name: Signal Person and Crane Operator observation
controls.barrier_role: Manual detection of boom approaching maximum angle limits
controls.lod_basis: Human observation as final line of defense before boom overtravel

supporting_text: Neither the Signal Person nor the Crane Operator noticed the boom was nearing the boom stops.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
